name: Mullá Muḥammad-i-Qá’iní
summary: An eminent Bahá'í scholar (c. 1829–1892) titled Nabíl-i-Akbar by Bahá'u'lláh, who carried the Faith to Samarqand and Bukhárá; posthumously named a Hand of the Cause by 'Abdu'l-Bahá and an Apostle of Bahá'u'lláh, for whom the Lawḥ-i-Ḥikmat was revealed.
importance_reason: Apostle of Bahá'u'lláh and Hand of the Cause; erudite teacher who opened Central Asia, recipient of a major Tablet.
